explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 25 from a base nutrition score of 34. Context dragged it down by 9 points.

topPros.title: unusually protein-forward for a seed snack
topPros.explanation: That gives it some practical appeal versus lighter snack foods.

topPros.title: has clear crunchy snack appeal
topPros.explanation: Easy to understand and package as a snack food.

topPros.title: still brings some useful minerals
topPros.explanation: Not devoid of nutritional upside despite the weaker category fit.

topCons.title: salt load is a major practical negative
topCons.explanation: The roasted salted format brings a clear real-world downside.

topCons.title: weak fibre density for the seed category
topCons.explanation: Seeds are supposed to dominate on fibre more convincingly than this.

topCons.title: fat profile is rough for a seed-category food
topCons.explanation: Too much saturated fat and not enough omega-3 support for this category.

narrationNotes: Context weakened the final tier case by 9 points.

closing.summary: unusually protein-forward for a seed snack and has clear crunchy snack appeal help watermelon Seeds (Roasted & Salted). but salt load is a major practical negative and weak fibre density for the seed category drag it down hard. best only as a supporting add-on rather than something to lean on heavily.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only as a supporting add-on rather than something to lean on heavily.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
